reasoning: While Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang recently claimed on a podcast in March 2026 that "we have achieved AGI," the resolution criteria strictly require a representative of a national government to publicly declare it as a formal announcement or major claim of achievement. Searches show no government officials—including those in the US, China, and the UAE—have echoed this statement or made their own declarations of having achieved AGI in 2026. US policy documents and White House announcements regarding AI and the newly formed PCAST advisory council (which includes Huang) focus heavily on maintaining leadership against China, formulating regulations, and advancing infrastructure, explicitly portraying AGI as a future goal rather than a completed milestone. The crowd forecast on April 2 was 1%, reflecting extreme skepticism that any government will officially adopt such a controversial and consequential claim in the short ~3.5 month window remaining until July 31, 2026. Given the massive geopolitical, strategic, and regulatory implications of a government formally claiming AGI, the probability remains very low, bounded at my lowest allowed estimate of 5% to account for unpredictable political rhetoric.
